mission: Turn the validated external dependency inventory into one deterministic integration priority model that separates mock-now execution from later live-provider strategy, without weakening proof, ambiguity, degraded-mode, or control-plane law.

summary.phase0_entry_reason: Planning and architecture foundation are frozen enough to open external-readiness work, but actual Phase 0 entry remains withheld because the current-baseline external-readiness gate is still blocked by onboarding, assurance, and simulator-freeze dependencies.

task_groups.label: Scorecards And Secret Posture
task_titles: external define provider selection scorecards for identity telephony notifications gp and pharmacy
external create sandbox account strategy and secret ownership model
task_groups.why_now: Freeze selection criteria, secret classes, and owner law before live onboarding scripts or vendor consoles appear.

task_groups.label: NHS Login Long Lead
task_titles: external use Playwright or other appropriate tooling browser automation to submit nhs login partner access requests
external use Playwright or other appropriate tooling browser automation to capture nhs login credentials and redirect uris
task_groups.why_now: NHS login is the only current-baseline identity rail that stays on the critical path for authenticated and recovery-grade patient authority.

task_groups.label: NHS And Partner Approvals
task_titles: external use Playwright or other appropriate tooling browser automation to submit im1 pairing prerequisite forms and track scal artifacts
external use Playwright or other appropriate tooling browser automation to request optional pds fhir sandbox access and feature flag plan
external use Playwright or other appropriate tooling browser automation to request mesh test mailbox and message workflow access
external use Playwright or other appropriate tooling browser automation to request nhs app sandpit and aos access
external use Playwright or other appropriate tooling browser automation to register nhs app site link metadata placeholders
task_groups.why_now: Start long-lead NHS, interoperability, and deferred-channel approvals without letting those tracks erase the mock-first execution posture.

task_groups.label: Commercial Vendor Onboarding
task_titles: external select telephony ivr sms and email vendors
external use Playwright or other appropriate tooling browser automation to create telephony vendor accounts and test numbers
external use Playwright or other appropriate tooling browser automation to create sms and email notification projects
external select transcription and artifact scanning providers
external use Playwright or other appropriate tooling browser automation to provision transcription and malware scanning projects
task_groups.why_now: Telephony, notifications, transcription, and scanning need scorecards and account plans, but live credentials may trail the simulator-first engineering path.

task_groups.label: Provider Discovery And Freeze
task_titles: external identify gp system provider sandbox paths and booking capability evidence
external identify pharmacy directory and update record access paths
external create local adapter simulator backlog for unavailable or manual integrations
external document manual approval checkpoints and browser automation retries
external freeze integration contract assumptions and degraded mode defaults
task_groups.why_now: Provider-path discovery, simulator backlog, manual checkpoints, and degraded-mode defaults turn raw dependency awareness into executable delivery law.

risk_summary.risk_title: Telephony evidence inadequacy
risk_summary.problem_statement: Recording, transcript, or structured capture failure could leave clinically material phone evidence unresolved while the workflow appears routine.

risk_summary.risk_title: Duplicate suppression or merge hazard
risk_summary.problem_statement: Cross-channel duplicate handling or same-request attach could inappropriately merge clinically distinct evidence or hide replay collisions.

risk_summary.risk_title: Urgent diversion under-triage or over-triage
risk_summary.problem_statement: Rule or evidence changes could request urgent diversion too late, too early, or settle it inaccurately across channels.

risk_summary.risk_title: NHS login partner onboarding or redirect proof delay stalls the current baseline
leading_indicators: NHS login partner onboarding task still marked blocked
redirect URI inventory differs across environments
subject-binding proof remains mock-only while current-baseline tasks depend on it
risk_summary.problem_statement: The current baseline assumes NHS login onboarding, redirect inventory, and subject-binding proof arrive before wider patient-authenticated flows open.

risk_summary.risk_title: Notifications, telephony, transcription, or scanning onboarding drifts past the engineering path
leading_indicators: manual checkpoint debt grows without owner
secure-link or callback fallback posture remains unresolved
risk_summary.problem_statement: Telephony, notification, transcription, and malware-scanning suppliers are long-lead delivery dependencies with safety and disclosure impact.

risk_summary.risk_title: IM1 and SCAL readiness lag blocks Phase 0 assurance merge and later booking reach
leading_indicators: IM1 or SCAL packs remain draft-only
interop workstream artifacts are stale at gate time
risk_summary.problem_statement: IM1 prerequisite and SCAL readiness must be visible before Phase 0 claims interoperability readiness.

risk_summary.risk_title: continuity-sensitive calmness is proven by ExperienceContinuityControlEvidence
leading_indicators: Reject summaries that describe calm continuity behavior without naming the continuity evidence tuple that proves it.
risk_summary.problem_statement: Treat patient-home actionability, thread settlement, booking manage, support replay, assistive session, workspace completion, and pharmacy-console settlement as evidence-producing continuity controls with shared ops and governance consumption.

risk_summary.risk_title: ExternalConfirmationGate governs ambiguous booking and dispatch truth
leading_indicators: Reject summary language that implies authoritative external success before confirmation-gate resolution.
risk_summary.problem_statement: Use `ExternalConfirmationGate` and case-local truth projections for ambiguous external booking or dispatch states; never imply final booked or referred calmness before the gate clears.

risk_summary.risk_title: Pharmacy directory, transport, or outcome observation posture remains weak
leading_indicators: directory or transport onboarding remains onboarding-only at Phase 6 entry
pharmacy outcome observation path still lacks authoritative proof
manual outcome reconciliation debt remains open
risk_summary.problem_statement: Directory truth, dispatch transport, and outcome observation must all line up before pharmacy calmness or closure can be treated as trustworthy.

risk_summary.risk_title: CommandSettlementRecord is the authoritative outcome for visible mutation state
leading_indicators: Reject summary or UI contracts that collapse local acknowledgement into final authoritative outcome.
risk_summary.problem_statement: UI calmness, success, stale recovery, and continuation posture must advance from authoritative `CommandSettlementRecord`, not optimistic UI or delivery-only signals.

risk_summary.risk_title: pharmacy calmness depends on dispatch proof, not transport acceptance alone
leading_indicators: Fail pharmacy summary text that equates send acceptance with final referral truth.
risk_summary.problem_statement: Separate transport acceptance, provider acceptance, and authoritative dispatch proof, and keep patient or staff calmness pending until dispatch proof and any confirmation gate satisfy the transport class.

risk_summary.risk_title: RuntimePublicationBundle and DesignContractPublicationBundle publish one coherent surface tuple
leading_indicators: Fail any summary or publication contract that treats token export or route manifests as separable from the live runtime tuple.
risk_summary.problem_statement: Treat runtime publication, parity, route contracts, design contracts, and surface bindings as one exact published tuple before calm or writable posture remains live.

risk_summary.risk_title: Wrong-patient binding or correction failure
risk_summary.problem_statement: Identity binding, secure-link redemption, or correction flow could expose or act on the wrong patient if freezes, evidence, and release ordering fail.

risk_summary.risk_title: bounce-back, reopen, and fallback stay inside the same lineage with explicit recovery truth
risk_summary.problem_statement: When accepted progress degrades, reopen or bounce back inside the same lineage using explicit fallback, exception, or recovery cases rather than detached secondary workflows.

risk_summary.risk_title: reservation truth comes from CapacityReservation and ReservationTruthProjection
leading_indicators: Reject booking summaries that imply exclusivity without reservation-truth contracts.
risk_summary.problem_statement: Visible booking exclusivity and hold truth come from `ReservationAuthority`, `CapacityReservation`, and `ReservationTruthProjection`, not countdown copy or supplier hints.

risk_summary.risk_title: MESH and secure-message readiness lag blocks cross-organisation messaging truth
leading_indicators: MESH mailbox or secure-message onboarding remains blocked
hub or support messaging paths rely on generic email fallback only
risk_summary.problem_statement: Cross-organisation messaging readiness is a named dependency for hub and support flows, not a later implementation detail.

risk_summary.risk_title: CommandActionRecord is the canonical mutation envelope
leading_indicators: Fail route summaries that mention mutation without a durable action-envelope record.
risk_summary.problem_statement: Every consequence-bearing post-submit mutation emits one durable `CommandActionRecord` tied to the active route-intent tuple and governing object version.

risk_summary.risk_title: SubmissionEnvelope.state owns draft and pre-promotion lifecycle
leading_indicators: Reject any canonical schema or summary text that stores draft state on `Request.workflowState`.
risk_summary.problem_statement: Use `SubmissionEnvelope.state = draft | evidence_pending | ready_to_promote | promoted | abandoned | expired`; do not model draft as `Request.workflowState`.

risk_summary.risk_title: Booking supplier capability evidence remains ambiguous too late in the programme
leading_indicators: waitlist or confirmation fallback paths remain assumption-only
booking merge gate depends on simulator-only truth late in the cycle
risk_summary.problem_statement: Booking implementation can outrun real supplier capability evidence, leaving slot truth, confirmation, or fallback semantics under-specified.

risk_summary.risk_title: identityState plus nullable patientRef derived from IdentityBinding
leading_indicators: Reject any summary or schema that assumes unconditional `patientRef` or uses `ownershipState` instead of `identityState`.
risk_summary.problem_statement: Use `identityState = anonymous | partial_match | matched | claimed`; treat `patientRef` as nullable and derive it only from settled `IdentityBinding`.

risk_summary.risk_title: RouteIntentBinding is the canonical post-submit mutation fence
leading_indicators: Reject patient or staff mutation summaries that describe live actions without a bound route-intent tuple.
risk_summary.problem_statement: Every post-submit mutation binds one live `RouteIntentBinding` over route family, session, subject binding, fence epoch, and publication posture.

risk_summary.risk_title: child domains emit milestones and evidence; they do not write canonical Request state directly
leading_indicators: Reject summary text or contracts that let a child domain claim final canonical request milestone ownership.
risk_summary.problem_statement: Normalize triage, booking, hub, and pharmacy outputs to case-local milestones, gates, and leases that `LifecycleCoordinator` consumes to derive canonical request state.

risk_summary.risk_title: channel profiles constrain shell posture without redefining the shell family
leading_indicators: Reject summary language that treats NHS App embedded delivery as a current baseline hard gate or separate native workflow.
risk_summary.problem_statement: Embedded, constrained-browser, and browser handoff change channel posture and affordances, but not the owning shell family; NHS App remains a deferred channel-expansion phase rather than a present hard gate.

divergence_register.integration_name: NHS standards and assurance source watch
divergence_register.mock_now_summary: The external-readiness programme needs a pinned standards watch immediately so the rest of the provider strategy does not drift onto stale guidance or undocumented approval assumptions.
divergence_register.actual_provider_summary: There is no provider-acquisition track here; later work is watch refresh and exception handling, not a separate onboarding exercise.
divergence_register.proof_gap_summary: Maintain one pinned version map, freshness digest, and change-watch signal so new docs or standards changes never silently widen capability or compliance claims.
later_task_titles: external document manual approval checkpoints and browser automation retries
external freeze integration contract assumptions and degraded mode defaults
divergence_register.notes: The family is read-mostly, but it is still a baseline requirement because stale standards can invalidate the rest of the ranking.

divergence_register.integration_name: Pharmacy outcome observation and reconciliation seam
divergence_register.mock_now_summary: Outcome replay, weak correlation, manual review, and reopened-for-safety behavior are canonical control problems and must be testable before the live assured observation path exists.
divergence_register.actual_provider_summary: Real Update Record or equivalent inbound observation routes need assured combinations and correlation evidence later, but they do not remove the need for a replay-safe mock seam now.
divergence_register.proof_gap_summary: Simulate exact replay, semantic replay, collision review, unresolved match, resolved_apply, resolved_reopen, unmatched, proof-bearing correlation chains, and blocked closure. Weakly matched outcomes may not auto-close the request or stand in for authoritative proof.
later_task_titles: external define provider selection scorecards for identity telephony notifications gp and pharmacy
external identify pharmacy directory and update record access paths
external create local adapter simulator backlog for unavailable or manual integrations
external document manual approval checkpoints and browser automation retries
external freeze integration contract assumptions and degraded mode defaults
divergence_register.notes: Outcome ingest is replay-safe or it is not safe. This family blocks closure until the reconciliation gate settles.

divergence_register.integration_name: Telephony capture, transcript, and artifact-safety backplane
divergence_register.mock_now_summary: Telephony parity is baseline law and the product cannot wait for live carrier, recording, transcript, or scanning accounts before it proves IVR choreography, evidence readiness, and urgent fallback states.
divergence_register.actual_provider_summary: Live numbers, recording retention, transcript handling, and scanner placement all carry contract, privacy, and safety review before they become production truth.
divergence_register.proof_gap_summary: Simulate call-session creation, IVR branch capture, webhook retries, recording present or missing, transcript usable versus unusable, artifact quarantine, safety-usable versus manual-review states, callback or secure-link fallback, and urgent escalation. Answered call or file stored may not imply clinically usable evidence or completed contact.
later_task_titles: external define provider selection scorecards for identity telephony notifications gp and pharmacy
external create sandbox account strategy and secret ownership model
external select telephony ivr sms and email vendors
external use Playwright or other appropriate tooling browser automation to create telephony vendor accounts and test numbers
external select transcription and artifact scanning providers
external use Playwright or other appropriate tooling browser automation to provision transcription and malware scanning projects
external create local adapter simulator backlog for unavailable or manual integrations
external document manual approval checkpoints and browser automation retries
external freeze integration contract assumptions and degraded mode defaults
divergence_register.notes: Treat telephony capture as a proof-bearing evidence lane, not as a commodity voice API. Transcript completion alone is never enough; the readiness assessment stays authoritative.

divergence_register.mock_now_summary: Hub choice, callback fallback, practice visibility debt, and acknowledgement overdue states must be available to product and platform teams before real partner feeds or practice routes exist.
divergence_register.actual_provider_summary: Live partner feeds and practice acknowledgements still need data-sharing and trust review before they can drive patient-visible offers or closable hub outcomes.
divergence_register.proof_gap_summary: Simulate trusted versus stale capacity snapshots, callback fallback, return-to-practice, acknowledgement generations, overdue practice debt, and supplier drift without letting stale feeds or missing ack clear the case.
later_task_titles: external define provider selection scorecards for identity telephony notifications gp and pharmacy
external identify gp system provider sandbox paths and booking capability evidence
external create local adapter simulator backlog for unavailable or manual integrations
external document manual approval checkpoints and browser automation retries
external freeze integration contract assumptions and degraded mode defaults
divergence_register.notes: Hub flows must stay subordinate to trusted_offerable capacity and current-generation practice visibility debt.

divergence_register.integration_name: Pharmacy dispatch proof and urgent-return seam
divergence_register.mock_now_summary: The pharmacy loop cannot wait for live dispatch or monitored urgent-return routes before it proves frozen package hashes, transport ambiguity, redispatch, and urgent reopen behavior.
divergence_register.actual_provider_summary: Real monitored routes, transport assurance, and professional escalation contacts are later onboarding work, but the simulator must already preserve their proof versus ambiguity law.
divergence_register.proof_gap_summary: Simulate dispatch attempts, transport accepted versus authoritative proof, proof deadlines, redispatch, weak email or weak mailbox returns, urgent return phone escalation, and reopen-for-safety. Update Record may not stand in for urgent return and mock send acceptance may not count as resolved dispatch truth.
later_task_titles: external define provider selection scorecards for identity telephony notifications gp and pharmacy
external identify pharmacy directory and update record access paths
external create local adapter simulator backlog for unavailable or manual integrations
external document manual approval checkpoints and browser automation retries
external freeze integration contract assumptions and degraded mode defaults
divergence_register.notes: Low mock feasibility does not excuse low fidelity. This family is exactly where proof-bearing and simple transport semantics must not be flattened.

divergence_register.integration_name: Local booking provider capability and confirmation-truth seam
divergence_register.mock_now_summary: Booking revalidation, confirmation ambiguity, waitlist fallback, and manage-freeze rules must be proven behind a simulator before any supplier path, pairing, or portal credential becomes current.
divergence_register.actual_provider_summary: Live patient-facing booking still needs provider-path evidence, pairing readiness, and exact confirmation proof before it can move beyond simulator-backed development.
divergence_register.proof_gap_summary: Simulate slot search snapshots, capability resolution, held versus non-exclusive reservation truth, revalidation failure, confirmation_pending, disputed callbacks, same-commit read-after-write proof, manage freeze, waitlist fallback, and wrong-patient freeze. Queue or webhook acceptance may not imply booked truth.
later_task_titles: external define provider selection scorecards for identity telephony notifications gp and pharmacy
external use Playwright or other appropriate tooling browser automation to submit im1 pairing prerequisite forms and track scal artifacts
external identify gp system provider sandbox paths and booking capability evidence
external create local adapter simulator backlog for unavailable or manual integrations
external document manual approval checkpoints and browser automation retries
external freeze integration contract assumptions and degraded mode defaults
divergence_register.notes: Proof-bearing family. Confirmation truth must stay distinct from acceptance, queueing, or webhook arrival.

divergence_register.mock_now_summary: Patient choice, warned-choice explanations, opening-hours logic, and consent supersession need a stable directory simulator long before a real strategic search rail is current.
divergence_register.actual_provider_summary: Live directory access and source-freshness proof remain later onboarding work and should not be treated as a current-baseline blocker while the choice contract is still being built.
divergence_register.proof_gap_summary: Simulate snapshot freshness, provider opening hours, provider withdrawal, no-safe-choice conditions, warned choices, and consent reset when the selected provider or pathway drifts.
later_task_titles: external define provider selection scorecards for identity telephony notifications gp and pharmacy
external identify pharmacy directory and update record access paths
external create local adapter simulator backlog for unavailable or manual integrations
external document manual approval checkpoints and browser automation retries
external freeze integration contract assumptions and degraded mode defaults
divergence_register.notes: Directory drift may supersede the choice tuple, but it may not silently reorder or reinterpret a frozen patient choice.

divergence_register.mock_now_summary: Receipts, secure-link recovery, and callback or message reassurance need realistic delivery evidence and bounce semantics before a live notification vendor is onboarded.
divergence_register.actual_provider_summary: Live sender-domain verification, webhook ownership, and delivery evidence still need later onboarding before the system can promote calm patient or staff reassurance from a real provider.
divergence_register.proof_gap_summary: Simulate accepted, queued, delivered, bounced, disputed, and expired delivery chains and keep those states separate from current CommunicationEnvelope truth or secure-link authority.
later_task_titles: external define provider selection scorecards for identity telephony notifications gp and pharmacy
external create sandbox account strategy and secret ownership model
external select telephony ivr sms and email vendors
external use Playwright or other appropriate tooling browser automation to create sms and email notification projects
external create local adapter simulator backlog for unavailable or manual integrations
external document manual approval checkpoints and browser automation retries
external freeze integration contract assumptions and degraded mode defaults
divergence_register.notes: Do not let notification acceptance outrank booking, hub, or pharmacy truth. Delivery evidence informs communication posture only; it does not settle operational truth on its own.

divergence_register.integration_name: Cross-organisation secure messaging and MESH seam
divergence_register.mock_now_summary: Hub, practice, and pharmacy message flows need replay-safe transport proof, ambiguity, and escalation behavior now even though live mailbox or certificate onboarding will lag.
divergence_register.actual_provider_summary: Real mailboxes, certificates, minimum-necessary payload review, and cross-org approvals are later work that should start early but must not stall simulator-backed implementation.
divergence_register.proof_gap_summary: Simulate ordered outbox dispatch, queue acceptance, mailbox receipt, duplicate or reordered callbacks, disputed delivery, proof upgrades, and escalation routes. Transport acceptance alone may never settle hub or pharmacy truth, and the mock must preserve explicit proof versus ambiguity states for every effect chain.
later_task_titles: external define provider selection scorecards for identity telephony notifications gp and pharmacy
external create sandbox account strategy and secret ownership model
external use Playwright or other appropriate tooling browser automation to request mesh test mailbox and message workflow access
external create local adapter simulator backlog for unavailable or manual integrations
external document manual approval checkpoints and browser automation retries
external freeze integration contract assumptions and degraded mode defaults
divergence_register.notes: Proof-bearing transport family. Weak mailbox or weak transport semantics must stay visibly distinct from business settlement.

divergence_register.mock_now_summary: Phase 2 cannot defer auth transactions, route-intent binding, subject mismatch handling, or read-only versus writable recovery states until the partner onboarding completes.
divergence_register.actual_provider_summary: Current-baseline authenticated and recovery-grade patient authority still depends on real redirect inventory, partner approval, and live session proof before the rail is admissible.
divergence_register.proof_gap_summary: Simulate frozen authorize and callback scope bundles, state or nonce or PKCE fences, consent decline, insufficient assurance, subject mismatch, callback replay, auth_read_only, claim_pending, writable, and bounded recovery. Mock callback arrival must never equal writable authority.
later_task_titles: external define provider selection scorecards for identity telephony notifications gp and pharmacy
external create sandbox account strategy and secret ownership model
external use Playwright or other appropriate tooling browser automation to submit nhs login partner access requests
external use Playwright or other appropriate tooling browser automation to capture nhs login credentials and redirect uris
external document manual approval checkpoints and browser automation retries
external freeze integration contract assumptions and degraded mode defaults
divergence_register.notes: Core identity rail. Do not let callback success, subject claims, or token exchange stand in for SessionEstablishmentDecision plus RouteIntentBinding and current IdentityBinding.

divergence_register.integration_name: IM1 pairing and capability-governance prerequisite seam
divergence_register.mock_now_summary: The programme needs a governed capability-matrix seam and explicit blocked states now so booking and identity work stay decoupled from live supplier pairing while the long-lead onboarding runs.
divergence_register.actual_provider_summary: IM1 and SCAL readiness are still required before live supplier capability claims are admissible for booking reach, but the corpus explicitly keeps this out of the Phase 2 identity critical path.
divergence_register.proof_gap_summary: Simulate unsupported, supported-test-only, linkage-required, and blocked capability rows plus pairing-pack freshness states. The mock may not claim live supplier reach or patient-facing capability proof.
later_task_titles: external define provider selection scorecards for identity telephony notifications gp and pharmacy
external create sandbox account strategy and secret ownership model
external use Playwright or other appropriate tooling browser automation to submit im1 pairing prerequisite forms and track scal artifacts
external identify gp system provider sandbox paths and booking capability evidence
external document manual approval checkpoints and browser automation retries
external freeze integration contract assumptions and degraded mode defaults
divergence_register.notes: Long-lead baseline dependency. Keep IM1 out of the Phase 2 identity critical path even while starting the pairing programme early for later booking reach.

divergence_register.mock_now_summary: Only a thin no-op or fixture seam is required now so the identity model proves PDS is optional and cannot silently become the baseline truth source.
divergence_register.actual_provider_summary: PDS access carries legal-basis, approval, and feature-flag prerequisites and remains a later enrichment strategy rather than a current-baseline blocker.
divergence_register.proof_gap_summary: Model enrichment absent, enrichment available, partial demographic corroboration, and enrichment rejected without letting the seam widen identity authority or hide local matching uncertainty.
later_task_titles: external define provider selection scorecards for identity telephony notifications gp and pharmacy
external create sandbox account strategy and secret ownership model
external use Playwright or other appropriate tooling browser automation to request optional pds fhir sandbox access and feature flag plan
external freeze integration contract assumptions and degraded mode defaults
divergence_register.notes: Do not rank PDS above NHS login. The seam may enrich matching confidence later, but it does not replace append-only binding law or become a shortcut for ownership claims.

divergence_register.mock_now_summary: The internal continuation contract still needs a thin simulator so seeded versus challenge flows, delivery delay, and wrong-recipient safeguards are tested without making SMS a baseline dependency.
divergence_register.actual_provider_summary: SMS remains optional-flagged. Live sender registration and wrong-recipient governance should trail the current MVP proof and never outrank the core identity or booking truth seams.
divergence_register.proof_gap_summary: Simulate queued, delayed, bounced, and expired deliveries, seeded versus challenge continuation, and grant issuance without letting provider acceptance imply grant redemption or verified reachability.
later_task_titles: external define provider selection scorecards for identity telephony notifications gp and pharmacy
external select telephony ivr sms and email vendors
external use Playwright or other appropriate tooling browser automation to create sms and email notification projects
external document manual approval checkpoints and browser automation retries
external freeze integration contract assumptions and degraded mode defaults
divergence_register.notes: Optional flagged only. SMS delivery is external; continuation grants, route authority, and claim law remain internal and must continue to work without live SMS.

divergence_register.mock_now_summary: Only preserve manifest, bridge-capability, and downgrade contracts in a local simulator so future embedded work cannot violate current shell or release law.
divergence_register.actual_provider_summary: Phase 7 remains deferred channel expansion and may not become a current-baseline blocker or reorder the live-provider acquisition queue.
divergence_register.proof_gap_summary: Simulate embedded manifest present or missing, bridge capability denied, safe browser handoff, route-freeze fallback, and placeholder-only embedded visibility. The deferred channel may not imply current-baseline writability.
later_task_titles: external use Playwright or other appropriate tooling browser automation to request nhs app sandpit and aos access
external use Playwright or other appropriate tooling browser automation to register nhs app site link metadata placeholders
external freeze integration contract assumptions and degraded mode defaults
divergence_register.notes: Deferred by programme law. Inventory it, sequence it, but do not let it block current-baseline execution.

divergence_register.mock_now_summary: Keep only bounded observe-only and placeholder-only seams alive so core clinical and operational delivery stays complete without any model-vendor dependency.
divergence_register.actual_provider_summary: Future assistive onboarding depends on intended-use decisions, medical-device boundary review, supplier assurance, and rollout cohorts that remain optional to core completeness.
divergence_register.proof_gap_summary: Simulate observe_only, shadow_only, frozen, placeholder_only, and hidden states. Assistive output may not become authoritative action, closure, or patient reassurance in the mock.
later_task_titles: external freeze integration contract assumptions and degraded mode defaults
divergence_register.notes: Optional to core completeness. Do not let model-vendor glamour or roadmap curiosity displace current-baseline need.

integration_families.why_mock_now: Phase 2 cannot defer auth transactions, route-intent binding, subject mismatch handling, or read-only versus writable recovery states until the partner onboarding completes.
integration_families.why_actual_later: Current-baseline authenticated and recovery-grade patient authority still depends on real redirect inventory, partner approval, and live session proof before the rail is admissible.
integration_families.minimum_mock_fidelity: Simulate frozen authorize and callback scope bundles, state or nonce or PKCE fences, consent decline, insufficient assurance, subject mismatch, callback replay, auth_read_only, claim_pending, writable, and bounded recovery. Mock callback arrival must never equal writable authority.

seq_023 secret ownership and vault-ingest posture is published for client identifiers and signing material
seq_024 and seq_025 capture partner access requests plus environment-specific redirect inventory
GATE_EXTERNAL_TO_FOUNDATION remains blocked until live onboarding proof is current for the active environment
integration_families.notes: Core identity rail. Do not let callback success, subject claims, or token exchange stand in for SessionEstablishmentDecision plus RouteIntentBinding and current IdentityBinding.

integration_families.integration_name: Telephony capture, transcript, and artifact-safety backplane
integration_families.why_mock_now: Telephony parity is baseline law and the product cannot wait for live carrier, recording, transcript, or scanning accounts before it proves IVR choreography, evidence readiness, and urgent fallback states.
integration_families.why_actual_later: Live numbers, recording retention, transcript handling, and scanner placement all carry contract, privacy, and safety review before they become production truth.
integration_families.minimum_mock_fidelity: Simulate call-session creation, IVR branch capture, webhook retries, recording present or missing, transcript usable versus unusable, artifact quarantine, safety-usable versus manual-review states, callback or secure-link fallback, and urgent escalation. Answered call or file stored may not imply clinically usable evidence or completed contact.
minimum_live_readiness_conditions: seq_031, seq_032, seq_034, and seq_035 complete the vendor selection and account-setup path
seq_023 publishes secret ownership for account tokens, webhooks, phone numbers, and project identifiers
seq_039 freezes manual checkpoints for recording, transcript, and quarantine failure
seq_040 freezes degraded defaults so telephony never settles routine truth from weak evidence
integration_families.notes: Treat telephony capture as a proof-bearing evidence lane, not as a commodity voice API. Transcript completion alone is never enough; the readiness assessment stays authoritative.

integration_families.why_actual_later: Live patient-facing booking still needs provider-path evidence, pairing readiness, and exact confirmation proof before it can move beyond simulator-backed development.

source_dependency_names: Assistive model vendor and subprocessor family
integration_families.why_mock_now: Keep only bounded observe-only and placeholder-only seams alive so core clinical and operational delivery stays complete without any model-vendor dependency.
integration_families.why_actual_later: Future assistive onboarding depends on intended-use decisions, medical-device boundary review, supplier assurance, and rollout cohorts that remain optional to core completeness.
integration_families.minimum_mock_fidelity: Simulate observe_only, shadow_only, frozen, placeholder_only, and hidden states. Assistive output may not become authoritative action, closure, or patient reassurance in the mock.
minimum_live_readiness_conditions: A future assistive rollout must carry explicit intended-use and assurance approval
seq_040 preserves the non-authoritative default even while optional vendor strategy is documented
integration_families.notes: Optional to core completeness. Do not let model-vendor glamour or roadmap curiosity displace current-baseline need.

integration_families.integration_name: NHS standards and assurance source watch
source_dependency_names: NHS standards and assurance source set
integration_families.why_mock_now: The external-readiness programme needs a pinned standards watch immediately so the rest of the provider strategy does not drift onto stale guidance or undocumented approval assumptions.
integration_families.why_actual_later: There is no provider-acquisition track here; later work is watch refresh and exception handling, not a separate onboarding exercise.
integration_families.minimum_mock_fidelity: Maintain one pinned version map, freshness digest, and change-watch signal so new docs or standards changes never silently widen capability or compliance claims.
minimum_live_readiness_conditions: seq_039 records manual review checkpoints when a watched standard changes
seq_040 freezes current assumption and degraded defaults when standards drift is unresolved
integration_families.notes: The family is read-mostly, but it is still a baseline requirement because stale standards can invalidate the rest of the ranking.

assumptions: ASSUMPTION_021_001: baseline_role captures current execution need for seq_021-040, not the eventual production necessity of every live provider.

ASSUMPTION_021_002: current MVP evidence can proceed with simulator-first delivery for booking, hub, messaging, pharmacy, and telephony so long as proof, ambiguity, and degraded fallbacks remain explicit.
ASSUMPTION_021_003: standards-watch work is modelled inside the same board because stale guidance can invalidate later live-onboarding decisions even though it is not a provider onboarding path.
